instruction:
The scores of students on the SAT college entrance examinations at a certain high school had a normal distribution with mean $\mu=552.6$ and standard deviation $\sigma=27.9$.
(a) What is the probability that a single student randomly chosen from all those taking the test scores 558 or higher? ANSWER: [ANS]
For parts (b) through (d), consider a simple random sample (SRS) of 30 students who took the test. (b) What are the mean and standard deviation of the sample mean score $\bar x$, of 30 students? The mean of the sampling distribution for $\bar x$ is: [ANS]
The standard deviation of the sampling distribution for $\bar x$ is: [ANS]
(c) What z-score corresponds to the mean score $\bar x$ of 558? ANSWER: [ANS]
(d) What is the probability that the mean score $\bar x$ of these students is 558 or higher? ANSWER: [ANS]
